creator: thefloridaqueenie
caption:
Looking for things to do around Florida? Comment "Guide" and I'll send you my interactive Florida map and guide with over 1,000 spots to explore! I did the work for you so you can just hit the road without hours of research and stress!

Which sand do you prefer and why?? I personally love the sand at Siesta Key beach best because I love going on long walks and making dribble sand castles like I did when I was a kid! Plus the sand is so soft and cool to the touch!

#floridabeaches #beachsand #siestakeybeach #venicebeach #floridahiddengems

transcription.text: People are always asking me what the difference is between the sand at Siesta Key Beach and the sand at Venice Beach, so I'm going to explain why they're so different so you can plan your next vacation accordingly. The sand at beaches like Siesta Key is made from 99% quartz which is why it's so white, soft, and cool to the touch even when it's really hot outside. It even squeaks when you walk on it. This sand is hard packed near the water so you won't sink in when you're walking on it and the sand makes the best dribble sand castles. If you grew up going to Siesta Key Beach like I did, you probably made these every time you went to the beach. The sand at beaches like Venice Beach is made from a mixture of quartz and black fossil fragments such as shark's teeth. This sand is coarser and is gray when dry and almost black when it's wet. The sand gets very hot so be sure to bring shoes or else the walk back to the car will be rough. If you love hunting for shark's teeth, these beaches will score you handfuls every visit. Be sure to share this with someone else who is curious about the differences in sand in Florida.
